question_text: Radio waves and gamma rays are both types of electromagnetic radiation. Which statement correctly compares a property of these two types of radiation?

options.label: A
options.text: Radio waves have a higher frequency and carry more energy than gamma rays

options.label: B
options.text: Gamma rays have a higher frequency and carry more energy than radio waves

options.label: C
options.text: Radio waves and gamma rays have the same frequency but different wavelengths

options.label: D
options.text: Both types of radiation travel at different speeds through a vacuum

correct_answer: Gamma rays have a higher frequency and carry more energy than radio waves
explanation: The electromagnetic spectrum is ordered by frequency and wavelength. Gamma rays are at the high-frequency end and carry much more energy than radio waves, which are at the low-frequency end. The relationship is: higher frequency = higher energy. All electromagnetic waves travel at the same speed (3.0 × 10⁸ m/s) through a vacuum.
